objective: Implement the self-learning and self-healing loop slice from epic #224 by making documented mistake patterns available before worker dispatch, halting repeated known failures, capturing novel failures append-only, and surfacing stale or duplicate learning entries in the weekly sweep.
scope_boundary: Build deterministic pre-dispatch lookup from fail-fast logs, error patterns, closeouts, operator context, graphify attention, and the org compendium.
Allow worker packets to carry cited known-failure context with direct evidence paths.
Halt dispatch when known-failure context indicates a repeated mistake.
Add issue-backed append-only write-back for novel failure patterns without overwriting human-authored logs.
Add weekly duplicate and stale learning reporting.
out_of_scope: Calling cloud models or local models for semantic inference.
Automatically editing human-authored FAIL_FAST_LOG.md, ERROR_PATTERNS.md, or MEMORY.md.
Replacing graphify or compendium claims as evidence sources.
Changing product repos from this governance slice.
research_summary: Issue #230 requires known-failure context before workers repeat mistakes. Existing governance already has FAIL_FAST_LOG.md, ERROR_PATTERNS.md, raw closeouts, raw operator context, graphify outputs, compendium generation, memory integrity checks, and the packet queue from #229. This slice adds deterministic lexical lookup and append-only write-back around those sources. Graphify and compendium text are used only as attention/routing signals; packet context cites direct source files.

sprints.name: Learning Lookup
sprints.goal: Index prior mistake artifacts and inject cited context into packets.
tasks: Parse fail-fast logs, error patterns, closeouts, and operator context as direct evidence sources.
Use graphify and compendium tokens only for attention scoring.
acceptance_criteria: Known failure lookups include cited prior mistake paths.
Worker packets can carry known-failure context before dispatch.
Graphify inference does not become the evidence source for claims.

sprints.name: Halt And Write-Back
sprints.goal: Prevent repeated mistakes and capture novel failures append-only.
tasks: Update packet dispatch validation to halt repeated known failures.
Extend governance-surface planning classification for self-learning outputs.
acceptance_criteria: The same documented mistake twice halts dispatch or escalates planning.
Novel failures are captured without overwriting existing human-authored logs.
Self-learning output paths require issue-backed planning.

sprints.name: Weekly Reporting
sprints.goal: Surface stale and duplicate learning entries in the weekly sweep.
tasks: Append the self-learning markdown report to the overlord sweep issue.
Register docs, PDCAR, cross-review, closeout, graph/wiki, and compendium updates.
acceptance_criteria: Weekly sweep reports stale or duplicate learning entries.
Registries and data dictionary document the self-learning contract.
Alternate-family review is recorded before merge.

specialist_reviews.focus: Deterministic lookup, cited evidence paths, repeated-failure halt, append-only write-back, weekly reporting, and no model/payload execution.
specialist_reviews.summary: The implementation uses local deterministic parsing and reporting. It does not invoke model inference or overwrite existing human-authored learning logs.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review is required for Tier 1 governance dispatch and self-healing controls. Findings are recorded in the #230 cross-review artifact and must be resolved or accepted before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement #230 deterministic self-learning lookup, packet context injection, repeated-failure halt, append-only novel failure write-back, weekly reporting, tests, docs, review, and closeout only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Build self-learning controls, run validators, obtain alternate-family review, close out, open PR, and merge after checks pass.

material_deviation_rules: If model inference is required to classify a mistake, defer that behavior to a later issue.
If a learning write-back would overwrite human-authored logs, stop and write a new issue-backed artifact instead.
If graphify or compendium text is the only evidence for a claim, do not inject that claim into packets.
If repeated known failures are detected, halt dispatch or escalate to planning rather than proceeding.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, loop through, no HITL
